an1.10:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see any other single tangible that so completely possesses a woman's mind and stands in it as does, bhikkhus, a man's tangible.
an1.10:1.2: A man's tangible, bhikkhus, so completely possesses a woman's mind and stands in it.”
an1.10:1.4: The Chapter on Forms, and So On, the first.
an1.1:0.2: 1. The Chapter on Forms, and So On
an1.1:1.2: at one time the _Bhagavā_ was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta's Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika's park.
an1.1:2.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see any other single form that so completely possesses a man's mind and stands in it as does, bhikkhus, a woman's form.
an1.1:2.2: A woman's form, bhikkhus, so completely possesses a man's mind and stands in it.”
an1.2:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see any other single sound that so completely possesses a man's mind and stands in it as does, bhikkhus, a woman's sound.
an1.2:1.2: A woman's sound, bhikkhus, so completely possesses a man's mind and stands in it.”
